title: Zara's London Memories
description: A narrative lesson about culture shock and homesickness, with past tenses and reported speech.
summary_pt_br: Misturando past simple, continuous, perfect e reported speech para contar uma história em camadas.

scenes.subtitle_en: A narrative lesson about culture shock and homesickness, with past tenses and reported speech.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Misturando past simple, continuous, perfect e reported speech para contar uma história em camadas.

scenes.text_en: A narrative lesson about culture shock and homesickness, with past tenses and reported speech. Today's focus: zara's london memories. When I moved to Brazil, I had never tasted a real mango before.
scenes.text_pt: Misturando past simple, continuous, perfect e reported speech para contar uma história em camadas. Foco de hoje: zara's london memories. Quando me mudei pro Brasil, nunca tinha provado uma manga de verdade.

scenes.title: Zara's London Memories
scenes.body_en: Mixing past simple, past continuous, past perfect, and reported speech to tell a layered story.
scenes.body_pt: Misturando past simple, continuous, perfect e reported speech para contar uma história em camadas.

examples.en: When I moved to Brazil, I had never tasted a real mango before.
examples.pt: Quando me mudei pro Brasil, nunca tinha provado uma manga de verdade.

examples.en: My grandmother used to tell me that London was the center of the world.
examples.pt: Minha avó dizia que Londres era o centro do mundo.

examples.en: She said Brazil would change me — and she was right.
examples.pt: Ela disse que o Brasil me mudaria — e estava certa.

exercise.question: Complete: "When I moved to ___, I had never tasted a real mango before."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "When I moved to Brazil, I had never tasted a real mango before."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Quando me mudei pro Brasil, nunca tinha provado uma manga de verdade.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
My grandmother used to tell me that London was the center of the world.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "My grandmother used to tell me that London was the center of the world."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Minha avó dizia que Londres era o centro do mundo.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Zara's London Memories — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre zara's london memories. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Zara, what surprised you most when you arrived in Brazil?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The sunlight was so strong it hurt my eyes.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Did London feel gray after you moved?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I missed the gray and the rain for months.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What was the hardest part of adjusting?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The volume of conversations overwhelmed me.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Did your grandmother ever visit you here?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: She said she would come, but she never did.
turns.pt_hint: Disse que viria, mas não veio.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Do you still feel homesick sometimes?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Only when it rains at the wrong time of year.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Brazil is your home now. But London will always be your roots.

scenes.title: Zara remembers London
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write what Zara missed and what she loves now. Use past tenses and reported speech.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: On a rainy Tuesday afternoon, Zara sat by the window remembering her first months in Brazil.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: When I arrived, I had never felt heat like this before. It was almost frightening.

paragraphs.speaker: Grandmother
paragraphs.en: I told her that London was the center of the world. She needed to prove me wrong.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: My grandmother used to write me letters by hand every single week without fail.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: She said Brazil would change me forever, and that was the only prediction she got right.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Two countries, one heart — the kind of map that only the homesick learn to read.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Zara's London Memories". A narrative lesson about culture shock and homesickness, with past tenses and reported speech. When I moved to Brazil, I had never tasted a real mango before. My grandmother used to tell me that London was the center of the world. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Zara's London Memories". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Zara's London Memories".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
